title: About the course: TMA4269 Fall 2026
text:
Course description
Data science is about extracting knowledge from data. This includes identifying patterns, making predictions, communicating results, and supporting decision-making.
This course provides a practical and example-based introduction to fundamental topics such as data visualisation, regression models, and machine learning.

title: Welcome to the course TMA4269 Data Science
text:
The course TMA4269 Data Science is developed by the Department of Mathematical Sciences at NTNU, and intended as a ‘k-emne’ (complementary course) for 5-year integrated master of technology students (Siving). Other students are also welcome to the course. The course is given for the first time in the fall semester of 2026. Teachers are Sara Martino and Thea Bjørnland.
This page presents learning materials etc. Other details (schedule, messages etc) are posted at the course wikipages.

title: Welcome to the course TMA4269 Data Science
text:
The course TMA4269 Data Science is developed by the Department of Mathematical Sciences at NTNU, and intended as a ‘k-emne’ (complementary course) for 5-year integrated master of technology students (Siving). Other students are also welcome to the course. The course is given for the first time in the fall semester of 2026. Teachers are Sara Martino and Thea Bjørnland.
This page presents learning materials etc. Other details (schedule, messages etc) are posted at the course wikipages.

title: Welcome to the course TMA4269 Data Science
text:
Course description

Content
Data science is about extracting knowledge from data. This includes identifying patterns, making predictions, communicating results, and supporting decision-making. This course provides a practical and example-based introduction to fundamental topics such as data visualisation, regression models, and machine learning. We will discuss the limitations of data analysis and modeling to avoid misinterpretation or misuse of model results.


Learning outcomes
The student is familiar with the most common methods and algorithms for data visualisation, modeling, and prediction. The student can critically evaluate results from various data science processes. This includes understanding the limitations of models, as well as being able to discuss the implications of these with others.


Learning and assessment activities
For this course your grade is set using portfolio assessment. You will attend and participate in mandatory activities, and complete (in groups) two projects. All activities and project work are intended to take place during the lecture times.

title: About the course: TMA4269 Fall 2026
text:
Some context (1)

This is the first semester we run the course TMA4269 Data Science
This is also the first time we use portfolio assessment (mappevurdering)
This is also the first time we have built an entire course around applications and group discussions
This is also the first time we use Canvas

title: About the course: TMA4269 Fall 2026
text:
Some context (2)

The course was designed as a ‘k-emne’ (complementary course), which means:

Not technical / theoretical (if this is what you require -> TMA4268 Statistical Learning)
We focus on “apply, use, and discuss” data and methods for data analysis
We only require that you have taken a basic statistics course (e.g. TMA4240/45)

The course is also open to other students

title: About the course: TMA4269 Fall 2026
text:
Co-creation

The course has no strict curriculum
All learning activities and assessment types are new and being developed as we go
You will learn from us and we will learn from you

–> We all need to be a bit flexible

title: About the course: TMA4269 Fall 2026
text:
Teaching Excellence Program at NTNU (Toppundervisning)

We are developing the course through the NTNU teaching excellence program
“AI-assisted learning in statistics”

title: About the course: TMA4269 Fall 2026
text:
Portfolio assessment

Individual letter grades (A-F) are set based on your portfolio (“mappe”)
All elements of the portfolio are completed in groups
All the work is done during class hours
A “polished” hand-in does not count extra
We have a tentative plan on our wikipages -> lets have a look
